# Gate Drift Lab Notebook

Append-only evidence for LAB-A export-scan drift. Production validation behavior is out of scope for this lab.

## 2026-07-09 - Control exact; flowing-pump fixture not yet qualified

### Static control

- Path: export-only production `/export-platform`; serialized counts read from `storage.platform_exports[export_id].verification`.
- Provenance: export `009_gate-drift-a-control-1783641559933`, record/start tick `6888969`, observed completion tick `6889115`, span `146` ticks, `143` entities.
- Census mechanism: each physical census was computed and printed atomically in one `/sc` invocation.
- Fluid result: serialized `water=2000`; start physical `water=2000`; completion physical `water=2000`; maximum residual `0`.
- Item result: serialized `iron-plate=2`; start physical `iron-plate=2`; completion physical `iron-plate=2`; maximum residual `0`.
- Cleanup: both instances reported zero LAB-A surfaces, zero lab storage, game unpaused, zero destination holds, zero locked platforms, zero committed source tombstones, and zero LAB-A `storage.platform_exports` records.

Stored-verification equivalence: `ExportPipeline.complete()` attaches `job.export_data.verification` after the atomic belt scan, then stores that same table as the plaintext `storage.platform_exports[export_id].verification` sibling of the compressed payload. The uncompressed fallback exposes `.verification` at the same path.

### TRIED & SETTLED - pre-lock pump fixture failures

These are fixture-construction negatives, not export-lock findings. `freeze0` was not allowed to run because pre-lock cross-segment flow was not established.

| Attempt | Measured state | Conclusion |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Inline guessed ports | Powered active pump, but tanks and adjacent pipes exposed separate segment IDs; `water=20000` remained in the source tank. | Geometry invalid; no flowing specimen. |
| R7-offset layout | Source tank and some pipes connected after corner routing, but destination routing remained on a separate tank segment; pump transferred `0`. | Partial connectivity only; no flowing specimen. |
| Prototype-port layout | Source side and destination side formed two connected segments (`85` and `86`); pump was `active=true`, energy `533.3333333333334`, direction `4`, but source stayed `water=20000` and destination stayed empty over the observation window. | Pump stall cause unexplained; requires the Adjudication-3 diagnostic before any fixture change. |

All failed attempts completed the seven-field cleanup contract on both instances. No production or gate conclusion is drawn from these failures.

### Adjudication-3 pump diagnostic

One diagnostic-only pass used the unchanged prototype-port layout and read the engine's live state:

- `pump.status=defines.entity_status.working`; `active=true`; `energy=533.3333333333334`; `pumped_last_tick=0`.
- `pump.direction == defines.direction.east` was true (`4` on 2.0.77; the assertion used the define, not a numeric literal).
- `prototypes.entity["pump"].surface_conditions` was empty; the platform surface did not ignore conditions. The pump was not surface-restricted.
- Live output connection: intended target `(1641.5,100.5)`, `target_connected=true`.
- Live input connection: intended target `(1638.5,100.5)`, `target_connected=false`; the guessed input pipe was at `(1639.5,100.5)`.

Verdict: the pump was operational but had no connected fluid source. The single Adjudication-3 fixture change is dictated by the live connection API: move only the input pipe to the engine-reported target position. This diagnostic also completed the seven-field cleanup contract on both instances.

## 2026-07-10T00:50:08.583Z - LAB-A export-drift run (sections=control,freeze0,fluidflow,beltflow)

Stored-verification provenance: `ExportPipeline.complete()` attaches `job.export_data.verification` after the atomic belt scan, then stores that same table as the plaintext `storage.platform_exports[export_id].verification` sibling of the compressed payload; the uncompressed fallback exposes `.verification` at the same path.

## 2026-07-09 - Harness failure and superseded movement summaries

- A first corrected-fixture export crashed host-1 because the runner's error cleanup deleted the LAB-A surface while its async export still owned a live `LuaSurface` reference. `ExportPipeline.complete()` then reached the atomic ground-item scan with an invalid surface. Harness correction: cleanup now waits for zero prefix-matching `storage.async_jobs` before deleting any surface.
- Diagnostic fields pushed one helper-install command beyond the RCON comfort boundary. Harness correction: helper installation was split into three commands of approximately `5.4 KB`, `5.4 KB`, and `3.0 KB`, and belt evidence was compacted to an atomic signature plus sample rows.
- The preceding two full-run JSON blocks computed `locked_movement.fluids_changed=true` by including the final sample after the export had completed and the production lock had been released. Their raw samples show the actual lock-window state: `lock.present=true`, pump `status_name=disabled_by_script`, and both segment totals constant across every locked sample. Fluid totals changed only in the post-unlock sample after the pump returned to `working`.

The two preceding `fluids_changed=true` summary fields are therefore **INVALID/SUPERSEDED**. The runner now calculates movement and segment stability exclusively from samples satisfying `job_active=true`, `lock.present=true`, and `lock.kind="export"`. Two new full passes are required before LAB-A concludes.


## 2026-07-10T00:56:33.034Z - LAB-A export-drift run (sections=control,freeze0,fluidflow,beltflow)

Stored-verification provenance: `ExportPipeline.complete()` attaches `job.export_data.verification` after the atomic belt scan, then stores that same table as the plaintext `storage.platform_exports[export_id].verification` sibling of the compressed payload; the uncompressed fallback exposes `.verification` at the same path.

## 2026-07-10T01:00:06.706Z - LAB-A export-drift run (sections=control,freeze0,fluidflow,beltflow)

Stored-verification provenance: `ExportPipeline.complete()` attaches `job.export_data.verification` after the atomic belt scan, then stores that same table as the plaintext `storage.platform_exports[export_id].verification` sibling of the compressed payload; the uncompressed fallback exposes `.verification` at the same path.

## 2026-07-09 - LAB-A conclusion (valid evidence passes)

The two valid consecutive full passes are the blocks timestamped `2026-07-10T00:56:33.034Z` and `2026-07-10T01:00:06.706Z`. Earlier full-run movement summaries are explicitly superseded above.

- Pass 1 spans: control `154`, freeze0 `233`, fluidflow `240`, beltflow `231` ticks.
- Pass 2 spans: control `144`, freeze0 `240`, fluidflow `239`, beltflow `234` ticks.
- Every serialized-versus-physical comparison was exact at scan start and completion: maximum per-name fluid residual `0`; maximum per-name item residual `0`.
- In every flowing section, four samples were captured while `job_active=true` and the production export lock was present. The pump was `disabled_by_script`; per-segment fluid contents did not change; segment IDs stayed stable.
- Belts continued moving during the same production-lock samples, while the atomic belt scan preserved the exact `iron-plate=19` total.
- After lock release the pump returned to `working` and fluid moved again, confirming the fixture was live before and after the measured lock window.
- Both passes and the final standalone reset ended with all seven cleanup fields green on both instances: zero LAB-A surfaces, zero lab storage, game unpaused, zero destination holds, zero locked platforms, zero committed source tombstones, and zero LAB-A export records.

Classification under the owner parity contract: **residual approximately zero**. LAB-A measured no export-scan loss and no irreducible measurement residual in this fixture. This is measurement evidence only; gate/code decisions remain with the orchestrator.
